instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: Building a single class is useless.  It needs to be used in context.

runs.text: Building a single class is useless.  It needs to be used in context.

text_blocks.text: There are two main relationships between classes

runs.text: There are two main relationships between classes

text_blocks.text: “Joe’s Automotive Shop services foreign cars, and specializes in servicing cars made by Mercedes, Porsche, and BMW.  When a customer brings a car to the shop, the manager gets the customer’s name, address, and telephone number.  Then the manager determines the make, model, and year of the car, and gives the customer a service quote.  The service quote shows the estimated parts charges, estimated labor charges, sales tax, and total estimated charges.”

runs.text: “Joe’s Automotive Shop services foreign cars, and specializes in servicing cars made by Mercedes, Porsche, and BMW.  When a customer brings a car to the shop, the manager gets the customer’s name, address, and telephone number.  Then the manager determines the make, model, and year of the car, and gives the customer a service quote.  The service quote shows the estimated parts charges, estimated labor charges, sales tax, and total estimated charges.”

text_blocks.text: Source: Starting Out with Java: From Control Structures through Objects by Tony Gaddis

input.title: Advanced Input and Simple Exception Handling

text_blocks.text: Scanners actually have an inconveniently annoying flaw

runs.text: Scanners actually have an inconveniently annoying flaw

text_blocks.text: Some of you may have already encountered it
I couldn’t even enter my name before it finished

runs.text: I couldn’t even enter my name before it finished

text_blocks.text: Scanner has a buffering quirk.  When scanning for a token (an item like boolean, int) that is not a line, the scanner reads up to the new line character, but does not consume it.

runs.text: Scanner has a buffering quirk.  When scanning for a token (an item like
) that is not a line, the scanner reads up to the new line character, but does not consume it.

text_blocks.text: When you call nextLine(), it sees the new line, assumes it is done reading, and returns an empty String.
